sessions.intent: Plan how to make the builder's self-build reliable on nano via prompt slimming, schema-drift fix, formal-grammar pinning, and a scored subagent competition gated on an eval harness.

before.assumptions: Self-build flakiness on nano stems from oversized accreted always-on prompts (Plan.llm/Compile.llm), per-action prose misplaced into the stable prompt instead of the lazy os/system/modules/* markdown, and a prompt-vs-schema contradiction (QueryAndVerify schema lacks `modifiers` and types `type?:string` while prompts demand modifier-nesting + structured {name,kind,strict}). Bigger-model JsonParseError is consistent with that drift.
before.risk: A competition can optimize the builder into reliability while regressing ordinary builds (overfit). The eval is the load-bearing part; without a held-out generalization set and a pass-rate gate, scoring is gameable and noise-dominated (self-build is non-deterministic).

actions.context: Wrote the full plan (diagnosis A-D, levers 1-4, harness design, lexicographic scoring with pass-rate gate, execution sequence) so the multi-session harness work isn't lost. No prompt/code edits yet — planning only.

after.status: plan written; awaiting Ingi's go-ahead on corpus contents, N (trials), M (agents), and harness implementation medium before building the harness
after.notes: Levers split: competition scope = Plan.llm/Compile.llm/CompileUser.llm/os/system/modules/* (read-fresh, no bootstrap rebuild). Out of competition: schema fix #2 (.goal, needs rebuild) and formal-grammar pinning #3 (feeds #4 formal-as-sole-output, needs coder handoff).
